title_en: Psychedelic Therapy Crashed and Burned. MAHA Might Bring It Back
title_zh: 迷幻疗法一度重创并熄火，MAHA或许会使其重生

paragraphs.en: This was supposed to be the year of the MDMA revolution.
paragraphs.zh: 这本应是MDMA革命的一年。

paragraphs.en: About this time last year, prescription MDMA looked like a sure thing. After decades of clinical research, political wrangling, and aggressive promotion, the popular underground club drug was set to be tamed and medicalized, with a stamp of approval from the US Food and Drug Administration. Then, it wasn’t.
paragraphs.zh: 去年的这个时候，处方级MDMA看起来前景十分确定。经过数十年的临床研究、政治拉锯与激进宣传，这种在地下舞曲文化中流行的药物即将被驯化并医学化，并获得美国食品药品监督管理局的批准。随后，这一切都没发生。

paragraphs.en: In a stark change of course, the FDA rejected the MDMA therapy it had been considering by a 10-1 vote. The decision derailed psychedelic medicine for the foreseeable future.
paragraphs.zh: 在一次突然而激烈的转向中，FDA以10比1投票否决了它此前正在审议的MDMA疗法，这一决定使可预见的未来里迷幻药治疗事业受挫。

paragraphs.en: Except for one thing—an unexpected lifeline from the Trump administration. In May, the FDA’s new commissioner, surgical oncologist Marty Makary, appeared on cable news to declare MDMA and other Schedule 1 narcotics “a top priority for this FDA and this administration.” Elsewhere, Mr. MAHA himself, the US Health and Human Services secretary, Robert F. Kennedy Jr., has spoken positively about the psychoactive stems-and-bark tea ayahuasca. Matt Zorn, a lawyer recently appointed to RFK Jr.’s department, had previously fought the US government to allow access to cannabis and psychedelic mushrooms. Casey Means, Trump’s nominee for surgeon general, has spoken of the benefits of psilocybin-assisted therapy, claiming that psychedelic mushrooms helped her find love and made her feel like “part of an infinite and unbroken series of cosmic nesting dolls.”
paragraphs.zh: 但有一个例外——来自特朗普政府的意外支援。5月，FDA新任专员、外科肿瘤医生Marty Makary出现在有线新闻中，将MDMA和其他一类精神活性药物称为“本届FDA和本届政府的头等优先事项”。另一方面，MAHA本人、美国卫生与公共服务部部长Robert F. Kennedy Jr.也公开积极评价具有致幻作用的茎与树皮药茶ayahuasca。新近被任命到RFK Jr.部门的律师Matt Zorn过去曾向美国政府打官司，以争取对大麻和迷幻蘑菇的准入。特朗普提名的医学官长候选人Casey Means也谈到过裸盖菇素辅助疗法的益处，声称迷幻蘑菇帮助她找到了爱情，并让她有一种“自己像是属于一串永恒且不断衔接的宇宙套娃”之感。

paragraphs.en: Psychedelic medicine, as it turns out, slots rather comfortably into the burn-it-all ethos of RFK Jr.’s movement. But as MDMA’s advocates regroup to take advantage of this surge of support, they’re also reckoning with why they failed to win over the FDA—and whether a second attempt could go better. Could the psychedelic world’s new Trumpworld allies be the ones who finally help it achieve its goal?
paragraphs.zh: 事实证明，迷幻医学与RFK Jr.运动中“彻底摧毁旧秩序”的极端立场，其实并不冲突。随着MDMA倡导者借这股支持浪潮重新整合队形，他们也在反思：为什么当初没能赢得FDA的认可，以及二次尝试是否有机会做得更好。迷幻领域的新一批特朗普派盟友，是否可能成为最终帮助其达成目标的关键力量？

paragraphs.en: For almost half a century, American psychedelic medicine—and MDMA in particular—has had one indispensable advocate: Rick Doblin. On a cool December morning, I met Doblin at his bright purple craftsman home in the Boston suburbs. Dressed in a well-worn chamois shirt and khakis and with a wiry tangle of hair, he was cheery and avuncular. His look was classic New England and a bit bedraggled, befitting the scion of a wealthy industrialist family turned elder statesman of the counterculture.
paragraphs.zh: 近半个世纪以来，美国的迷幻医学，尤其是MDMA领域，一直有一位不可或缺的倡导者：里克·多布林（Rick Doblin）。在一个凉爽的12月清晨，我在波士顿郊区拜访了他位于他那间明亮紫色木屋般的屋宅。那天他穿着一件洗旧了的毛绒衬衫和卡其裤，头发蓬乱而紧凑，看起来和善而有长者般的亲切感。他的装扮很像新英格兰风格，却又有些蓬乱，像是出身于富裕工业家族的后代，最后却成为反文化阵营的元老。

paragraphs.en: Doblin first tried LSD in 1971 as a freshman studying psychology at Florida’s experimental New College. By 1982, he was studying under pioneering psychedelics researcher and therapist Stanislav Grof at the Esalen Institute in Big Sur, California. At the time, many at Esalen were excited about a legal chemical called MDMA, which was said to help people conquer fear and forge profound connections with others. Doblin sampled the drug with a girlfriend and was shocked at how easy it became to talk through their issues. But then, to Doblin’s chagrin, the US criminalized MDMA, and in 1986 he founded a nonprofit called MAPS—the Multidisciplinary Association for Psychedelic Studies.
paragraphs.zh: 多布林1971年在佛罗里达的一所实验性大学——新学院（New College）读心理学一年级时，第一次尝试了LSD。到1982年，他在加州比格梭尔的埃塞伦研究所（Esalen Institute）师从开创性的迷幻药研究者兼治疗师斯坦尼斯拉夫·格罗夫（Stanislav Grof）学习。当时，埃塞伦许多人都对一种名为MDMA的合法化化学物质很感兴趣，人们认为它能帮助人们克服恐惧，并与他人建立深层连接。多布林与一位女友一起试用了这种药物，并对它如何让两人轻松谈开彼此问题感到震惊。可惜，不久之后，正如多布林所懊恼的那样，美国将MDMA列为非法物质；1986年，他创建了一个非营利组织，名为MAPS——多学科迷幻研究协会（Multidisciplinary Association for Psychedelic Studies）。

paragraphs.en: For the next several years, MAPS was a one-man operation. To advocate for MDMA’s legalization, Doblin collected reports from animal studies on the drug’s toxicity and lined up experts to argue with Drug Enforcement Administration officials on Phil Donahue. He slipped into Nicaragua during its civil war and provided the drug to clinicians to treat traumatized soldiers and civilians. He even subjected himself to excruciating spinal taps in an attempt to disprove a prevalent belief that MDMA depleted natural stores of serotonin.
paragraphs.zh: 此后几年里，MAPS几乎是由他一人推动运作的。为了推动MDMA合法化，多布林汇集了关于该药毒性及动物实验的报告，并组织专家在菲尔·多诺霍（Phil Donahue）节目录制现场与美国毒品管制局（DEA）的官员辩论。他在尼加拉瓜内战期间偷偷进入该国，并把该药提供给临床医生，用于治疗创伤严重的士兵和平民。甚至更极端的是，他还让自己接受了极其痛苦的腰椎穿刺，试图驳斥当时流行的观点：即MDMA会耗竭人体天然的血清素储备。

paragraphs.en: Doblin believed early on that it was essential to distance MAPS from the counterculture to gain mainstream credibility. (The organization’s first Psychedelic Science conference in 2010 instituted a “no tie-dye” rule for staff.) He set out to collect clinical evidence on the benefits of MDMA and other prohibited drugs. He then used those results to wage a campaign to change public opinion and ultimately end prohibitions. Between 2005 and 2017, MAPS refined a model for clinicians to administer MDMA to patients suffering from post-traumatic stress disorder, guide their experiences, and provide post-trip emotional support. Its 74-page manual stressed the importance of “inner healing intelligence”—a concept touted by Doblin’s mentor Grof, which holds that the human psyche has an “innate capacity” to heal itself.
paragraphs.zh: 多布林很早就意识到，要让 MAPS 获得主流可信度，必须与反文化圈子保持距离。（该组织在2010年的第一届“迷幻科学大会”就对工作人员实行了“禁穿扎染衣”规定。）他着手收集 MDMA 和其他被禁药物的临床益处证据，并利用这些结果发起一场改变公众舆论、最终结束禁令的运动。2005年至2017年间，MAPS 不断完善一套模型，让临床医生为创伤后应激障碍（PTSD）患者施用 MDMA，引导他们的体验，并在其“出行”（“trip”）后提供情绪支持。其 74 页的手册强调了“内在疗愈智能”的重要性——这是多布林的导师格罗夫提出的一个概念，认为人类心灵具有“与生俱来的自我疗愈能力”。

paragraphs.en: During this time, MAPS operated as a charity, taking in over $6.7 million in 2013 to fund its research and advocacy. The organization prided itself on living its values of drug destigmatization; it had a policy where employees could consult with their managers on “smokable tasks” that could be completed under the influence of drugs. (“I personally feel like strategizing and brainstorming goes better when I’m high,” Doblin explains.)
paragraphs.zh: 在此期间，MAPS 作为一个慈善机构运作，2013 年接收捐款超过 670 万美元，用于资助其研究与倡导活动。该组织以践行“去除毒品污名化”价值观为荣；它有一项制度，允许员工与主管商议一些“可抽烟任务”，即在药物影响下也能完成的工作。多布林解释道：“我个人感觉自己在‘嗨’的时候，更擅长策划和头脑风暴。”

paragraphs.en: But the cornerstone of Doblin’s plan—establishing MDMA’s efficacy in a clinical trial—required resources of a different magnitude. MAPS needed to raise more than $100 million for research and operational costs. So in 2014 the organization minted a for-profit subsidiary, which came to be known as Lykos Therapeutics.
paragraphs.zh: 但多布林计划的基石——在临床试验中证明 MDMA 的疗效——需要的是另一档次的资源。MAPS 需要为研究和运营成本筹集超过 1 亿美元。因此，2014年该组织成立了一个营利性子公司，后来被称为 Lykos Therapeutics。

paragraphs.en: Seeking to change the public perception of MDMA as a party drug, Doblin decided to focus on a sympathetic patient population: military veterans diagnosed with PTSD. MAPS formed partnerships with veterans’ groups and secured a $1 million donation in 2018 from the Mercer Family Foundation, run by Rebekah Mercer, the conservative donor some have dubbed “the First Lady of the alt-right.” Mercer was a notable departure from the most prominent psychedelic philanthropists, like the Silicon Valley venture capitalist and longtime MAPS donor George Sarlo, podcaster and lifestyle guru Tim Ferriss, and David Bronner, an executive who is currently the “cosmic engagement officer” of Dr. Bronner’s empire of “magic soaps.”
paragraphs.zh: 为了改变公众对 MDMA 作为“派对药物”的看法，多布林决定将重点放在一个更容易被理解和同情的患者群体：被诊断为创伤后应激障碍的军人退伍军人。MAPS 与退伍军人组织建立了合作关系，并在2018年获得了由雷贝卡·默瑟（Rebekah Mercer）所掌舵的 Mercer Family Foundation 赠与的 100 万美元捐款；她是一些人称为“另类右翼第一夫人”的保守派捐助者。默瑟的出现，意味着资助来源与当下最知名的迷幻类慈善捐助者不同，如硅谷风险投资家兼 MAPS 长期捐助者乔治·萨洛（George Sarlo）、播客主持人和生活方式导师蒂姆·费里斯（Tim Ferriss），以及大卫·布朗纳（David Bronner）——这位现任“宇宙参与官（cosmic engagement officer）”，负责管理“神奇香皂”帝国 Dr. Bronner’s。

paragraphs.en: In 2017, the FDA granted MAPS’ MDMA treatment a Breakthrough Therapy designation, providing a fast-track through the agency’s arduous trialing and review process. In December 2023, Lykos submitted an application to the FDA including data from hundreds of patients. The data showed that the treatment had a remarkable 70 percent success rate. An expedient review would follow.
paragraphs.zh: 2017年，FDA授予了MAPS的MDMA疗法“突破性疗法”资格，使其在该机构艰难的临床试验和审评流程中获得了快速通道。2023年12月，Lykos向FDA提交了一份申请，内含来自数百名患者的数据。数据显示，该疗法的成功率高达70%。因此，快速审评程序就会随之启动。

paragraphs.en: On June 4, 2024, Jonathan Lubecky, a 48-year-old ex-Marine and US Army sergeant, appeared via webcam before an FDA advisory committee and told the story of how MDMA had saved his life. Lubecky had deployed to Iraq in 2005 and completed a yearlong tour of duty. After he returned home, he was plagued by nightmares, anxiety, depression, and thoughts of suicide. Triggered by fireworks during a Fourth of July celebration, Lubecky hid in a closet with his service dog. He had a meltdown at Disney World after spotting a guest in Islamic garb with a backpack.
paragraphs.zh: 2024年6月4日，48岁的前海军陆战队员、美国陆军中士Jonathan Lubecky通过网络摄像头在FDA咨询委员会前作证，讲述了MDMA如何拯救了他的生命。Lubecky于2005年被派往伊拉克，完成了一年的服役期。回到美国后，他长期受噩梦、焦虑、抑郁和自杀念头困扰。美国独立日庆典中的烟火触发了他的症状，他曾躲进衣橱并带着他的服务犬。一次在迪士尼乐园，他看到一名背着背包、身着伊斯兰服装的游客后便情绪崩溃。

paragraphs.en: After exhausting the treatment options offered by Veterans Affairs, Lubecky had lost all hope. He was taking 42 prescription pills a day and had made five attempts on his life. Then, one day in 2014, an intern at a South Carolina hospital slid him a piece of paper scrawled with the words “Google mdma ptsd.” When he did, Lubecky came across a clinical trial for MDMA therapy, run by MAPS, that was enlisting veterans. Figuring he had nothing to lose, he signed up.
paragraphs.zh: 在用尽退伍军人事务部提供的治疗选择后，Lubecky已经失去了所有希望。他每天服用42片处方药，并且曾五次尝试自杀。后来，在2014年的某一天，南卡罗来纳州一所医院的一名实习医生给了他一张写着“Google mdma ptsd”字样的纸条。于是，Lubecky找到了由MAPS开展的一项为退伍军人招募受试者的MDMA疗法临床试验。考虑到自己已经别无选择，他便报名参加。

paragraphs.en: Under the psychoactive sway of MDMA and the attention of a therapist, Lubecky found himself opening up about his wartime horrors. And then he kept going. “I went in thinking I was going to talk about Iraq,” he says, “and I ended up talking about my whole fucking life.” The treatment, which consisted of three drug-assisted therapy sessions as well as regular therapy, “healed me,” Lubecky told the panel. Since then, he has even made multiple trips to the front lines of the Ukraine-Russia war as a volunteer, resupplying Ukrainian medical stockpiles. Lubecky also became a dedicated advocate for the clinical use of MDMA and served as a government liaison for MAPS between 2018 and 2023.
paragraphs.zh: 在 MDMA 的精神活性影响和治疗师的关注下，Lubecky 发现自己开始敞开心扉，谈起战争中的恐怖经历。然后他继续说了下去。“我进去时以为自己会谈伊拉克，”他说，“结果最后谈的是我他妈的整个人生。”Lubecky 对委员会说，这项治疗“治好了我”；它包括三次药物辅助治疗，以及常规心理治疗。从那以后，他甚至多次作为志愿者前往乌克兰-俄罗斯战争前线，为乌克兰医疗库存补给物资。Lubecky 也成了 MDMA 临床使用的坚定倡导者，并在 2018 年至 2023 年间担任 MAPS 的政府联络人。

paragraphs.en: Like Doblin, Lubecky was optimistic that the treatment would get approved. But during the proceedings, a statement was entered into the record on behalf of a woman named Meaghan Buisson. In 2014, Buisson was recruited into a Phase 2 clinical trial run by MAPS. While under the influence of MDMA, she said, she had been “blindfolded, gagged, pinned, cuddled, and caressed” by the therapists, a married couple. She had attempted suicide afterward, she said; a doctor later told her she had effectively been “drugged, raped, blamed, and held as a sex slave.”
paragraphs.zh: 和多布林一样，卢贝基起初也很乐观，相信这种疗法会获得批准。不过在听证过程中，记录中提交了一份一位名叫梅根·布伊森（Meaghan Buisson）的女性声明。2014年，布伊森参加了由 MAPS 进行的第二阶段临床试验。她说，在 MDMA 作用下，治疗师——一对已婚夫妇——“给她蒙上眼睛、塞住嘴、按住、抱住并抚摸她”。她随后试图自杀；后来一位医生告诉她，她实际上是在“被下药、被强奸、被指责，并被当作性奴隶对待”。

paragraphs.en: Another speaker, Neşe Devenot, a senior writing lecturer at Johns Hopkins University, recounted an incident in which a therapist pinned down a patient “as their distress escalated to the point of shouting, quote, ‘Go away. Get your effing hands away from me.’” In a 17-page document submitted to the FDA, Devenot alleged that the MAPS therapy wasn’t a scientific treatment at all: It was a “therapy cult,” comparable to NXIVM, the notorious sex-trafficking pyramid scheme.
paragraphs.zh: 另一位发言人、约翰霍普金斯大学高级写作讲师内塞·德沃诺（Neşe Devenot）讲述了一个事件：一名治疗师把一位患者按在地上，“直到患者的痛苦升级到要大喊：‘走开。把你那该死的手从我身上拿开。’”在一份提交给美国食品药品监督管理局（FDA）的17页文件中，德沃诺指控 MAPS 的疗法根本不是科学治疗，而是“一个治疗教派”，可与臭名昭著的性贩运金字塔骗局 NXIVM 相提并论。

paragraphs.en: When the hearing concluded, after some eight exhausting hours of testimony, Lubecky stepped onto the balcony of his Washington, DC, apartment, inhaled deeply, and yelled, “FUCK!” He was sure Buisson’s and Devenot’s accounts would doom the treatment’s chances of approval. Here, he believed, was a medical innovation that could save thousands of lives, and it had been torpedoed not by its usual opponents, like law enforcement agencies or opponents of drug legalization, but by warring factions of the psychedelic community itself. Or, as he described them, “a bunch of fucking hippies who fucked it up.”
paragraphs.zh: 听证会结束后，经过近八个小时的消耗性证词，卢贝基走到他在华盛顿特区公寓的阳台上，深深吸了一口气，然后大喊了一声：“操！”他确信，布伊森和德沃诺的说法会把这项治疗的批准希望彻底扼杀。他认为这里本有一项或可拯救数千生命的医学创新，却没有被通常的反对者——比如执法机构或反对毒品合法化的人——破坏，而是被迷幻社群内部各派系所“打爆”。也就是他所说的，“一群该死的嬉皮士把它搞砸了。”

paragraphs.en: Until fairly recently, the “psychedelic space” was a small and somewhat parochial collection of academics, research chemists, and recreational trippers, all loosely connected to the drug underground or the vestigial 1960s counterculture. Then, in 2018, author Michael Pollan published How to Change Your Mind, his bestselling account of the “psychedelic renaissance,” and helped popularize drugs like LSD, MDMA, psilocybin, and mescaline.
paragraphs.zh: 直到最近不久，“迷幻领域”还是一小撮、带点自我封闭色彩的人群：学者、研究化学家和休闲吸食者，他们都或多或少与毒品地下网络或残留的20世纪60年代反主流文化有联系。然后在2018年，作家迈克尔·波兰（Michael Pollan）出版了《如何改变你的想法》（How to Change Your Mind），这本关于“迷幻复兴”的畅销书帮助将 LSD、MDMA、裸盖菇素和麦司卡林等药物推向了更广泛的公众视野。

paragraphs.en: The community’s gatherings outgrew church basements and Holiday Inn ballrooms and relocated to glass-and-steel convention centers swarming with pharmaceutical salespeople and venture capitalists. To many in the left-wing, anticapitalist psychedelic scene, Neşe Devenot told me, it was like the evil Eye of Sauron from The Lord of the Rings had swiveled in their direction.
paragraphs.zh: 这个圈子的聚会不再局限于教堂地下室和希尔顿假日酒店的舞厅，而是搬到了充满制药销售人员和风险投资家的玻璃钢结构会展中心。对许多左翼、反资本主义的迷幻社群来说，Neşe Devenot 形容说，就像《指环王》里索伦之眼般邪恶的目光，转向了他们。

paragraphs.en: Devenot, who uses they/them pronouns, first took LSD as a freshman at Bard. It was “the most profound experience of my life,” they said. Until that point, they had been terminally shy and suffered from intrusive thoughts about dying. But under the influence of LSD, Devenot says, “the finality and fearfulness I associated with death disappeared.” They fell in with the community of researchers and enthusiasts in which Doblin was regarded as a pioneer. “Before this field became financialized,” Devenot told me, “it was a domain for a lot of weirdos and misfits … people looking for community and meaning and connection.”
paragraphs.zh: Devenot（她/他）在巴德大学大一时第一次服用 LSD。她（他）说，那是“我一生中最深刻的体验”。在那之前，他们长期极度害羞，并且一直被关于死亡的侵入性念头困扰。但在 LSD 的影响下，Devenot 表示，“我曾把死亡联想到的终结感和恐惧感消失了”。他们融入了一个由研究者和爱好者组成的社群，在那里 Doblin 被视为先驱。“在这个领域还没有被金融化之前，”Devenot 告诉我，“它更像是许多古怪人物和局外人的天地……人们在那里寻找社群、意义和连接。”

paragraphs.en: In 2018, Devenot joined an advocacy group called Psymposia, which was founded to advocate for drug policy reform. The group began working diligently to conduct policy research and rail against the corporate capture of psychedelia. A Psymposia cofounder named Brian Normand told me that he finds the incursion of Silicon Valley and Big Pharma into psychedelia “incredibly cheesy.” With open letters, articles, academic papers, podcasts, and voluminous social media posts, Psymposia called attention to abusive practitioners of psychedelic therapy and right-wing uses and abuses of mind-expanding compounds, among other topics. Early on, Psymposia and MAPS worked together. But a few years after MAPS spun off its for-profit arm, the alliance splintered.
paragraphs.zh: 2018 年，Devenot 加入了名为 Psymposia 的倡导组织，该组织成立的目标是推动毒品政策改革。该团体随后开始认真推进政策研究，并猛烈抨击迷幻药领域被大公司侵占的局面。Psymposia 的联合创始人之一 Brian Normand 告诉我，他认为硅谷和大制药公司进入迷幻药领域“非常庸俗”。通过公开信、文章、学术论文、播客以及大量社交媒体发文，Psymposia 将公众注意力引向了精神治疗中不道德的从业者，以及右翼对扩张意识化合物的使用与滥用等问题。最初，Psymposia 与 MAPS 一起合作过。但在 MAPS 将其营利分支剥离数年后，这种联盟发生了分裂。

paragraphs.en: In 2021, Psymposia and New York magazine coproduced an investigative podcast called Cover Story: Power Trip. One of its most damning episodes was deeply critical of MAPS and featured Meaghan Buisson—the woman whose story of assault came up in the FDA hearings.
paragraphs.zh: 2021 年，Psymposia 与《纽约》杂志联合制作了名为《Cover Story: Power Trip》的调查播客。其最具指控性的节目之一，严厉抨击了 MAPS，并邀请了 Meaghan Buisson 参与——正是她在 FDA 听证会上讲述了遭遇性侵的故事。

paragraphs.en: The podcast, along with accompanying video footage of her therapy, covered many of the details she would later recount to the FDA. Buisson had been homeless around the time she enlisted in the trial in 2014, which was conducted by two MAPS-trained clinicians, Richard Yensen and Donna Dryer. Buisson also alleged that Yensen, an unlicensed psychotherapist, had pursued a sexual and romantic relationship with her after the trial concluded. In 2018, Buisson filed a civil suit against Yensen, which was reportedly settled out of court, and lodged an ethics complaint with MAPS. In a statement, MAPS has said that Yensen confirmed having sexual contact with Buisson. Dryer relinquished her medical license. (Yensen did not respond to a request for comment. When asked for comment, Dryer pointed to a Canadian Medical Association rule banning doctors from commenting on anyone who has ever been a patient.)
paragraphs.zh: 这档播客连同她治疗过程中的视频片段，披露了她后来向美国食品药品监督管理局（FDA）复述的许多细节。布伊森（Buisson）在2014年参与该试验时恰逢无家可归，而这项试验由两名受 MAPS 培训的临床医生——理查德·扬森（Richard Yensen）和唐娜·德赖尔（Donna Dryer）——进行。布伊森还指控，试验结束后，扬森这名未持牌的心理治疗师与她发展了性和恋爱关系。2018年，布伊森对扬森提起了民事诉讼，据报道双方庭外和解，并向 MAPS 提交了伦理投诉。MAPS 在一份声明中表示，扬森已确认与布伊森有性接触。德赖尔放弃了行医执照。（扬森未回应置评请求。德赖尔在被问及时，则援引了加拿大医学协会的一项规定，该规定禁止医生对任何曾经的患者发表评论。）

paragraphs.en: “We never tried to downplay it,” says Michael Mithoefer, a MAPS clinical researcher and psychiatrist who authored its treatment manual.
paragraphs.zh: “我们从未想过淡化这件事，”MAPS 的临床研究员和精神科医生迈克尔·米托弗（Michael Mithoefer）说，他是该机构治疗手册的作者。

paragraphs.en: MAPS confirmed that ethical misconduct had taken place, notified the FDA, barred Yensen and Dryer from all future activities, and provided Buisson with about $11,000 for therapy. MAPS also told trial participants that if they had similar complaints, they could confidentially contact its compliance team or the study’s Institutional Review Board. MAPS researchers did, however, include the results from Buisson’s trial in three scientific articles published in the journal Psychopharmacology. “That was a mistake,” Mithoefer says. Psychopharmacology has since retracted the papers. Ultimately, MAPS said, Buisson’s was the only documented complaint of sexual abuse among hundreds of patients treated with MDMA.
paragraphs.zh: MAPS 承认发生了伦理失范行为，向 FDA 通报了此事，禁止扬森和德赖尔再参与任何未来活动，并为布伊森提供了约1.1万美元的治疗费用。MAPS 还告诉试验参与者，如果有类似投诉，可保密联系其合规团队或该研究的机构审查委员会。尽管如此，MAPS 的研究人员仍将布伊森的试验结果收入了《Psychopharmacology》杂志发表的三篇科学论文之中。“这是一个错误，”米托弗说。之后《Psychopharmacology》已撤回了这些论文。MAPS 最终表示，在数百名接受 MDMA 治疗的患者中，布伊森的投诉是唯一有据可查的性虐待指控。

paragraphs.en: It later turned out that at least six of the speakers who gave negative testimonies to the FDA had some social or professional ties with Psymposia. (Devenot notes that two of those six were not officially connected but rather were “fans” of the group.) This created a strange asymmetry in the proceedings. The positive testimonies were given by representatives of groups such as the Veterans of Foreign Wars, which represents more than 1 million people. The most disturbing accounts came from people loosely associated with a small advocacy organization that disagreed with some of MAPS's methods.
paragraphs.zh: 后来发现，至少有六位向 FDA 提供负面证词的发言者与 Psymposia 有某种社会或职业联系。（德弗诺指出，其中两位并非正式关联，而是该团体的“粉丝”。）这在程序上形成了一种诡异的不对称：积极证词来自像“外侨战争老兵协会”（Veterans of Foreign Wars）这类团体代表，而该组织代表超过100万成员。最令人不安的说法则来自与一个规模较小的倡导组织有松散关联的人士，这个组织不同意 MAPS 某些方法。

paragraphs.en: In 2024, Psymposia received $185,000 on behalf of the Sarlo Charitable Fund, recommended by Susie Sarlo, daughter of the aforementioned MAPS donor, George Sarlo. A MAPS board member and the Sarlo estate had previously been in dispute over alleged elder abuse of George. Susie Sarlo also filed a public comment with the FDA advisory committee, warning of “MDMA’s recognized use as a tool for exploitation.” Asked about Susie Sarlo’s contributions, Normand maintained that Psymposia is “nobody’s attack dog.” Devenot noted that Psymposia members had spent years developing their analyses and raising the same concerns, long before receiving funding.
paragraphs.zh: 2024年，Psymposia 代表 Sarlo 慈善基金会收到了 185,000 美元，捐款由前文提到的 MAPS 捐赠人乔治·萨洛（George Sarlo）的女儿苏茜·萨洛（Susie Sarlo）推荐。此前，一名 MAPS 董事会成员与萨洛家族遗产委员会曾就乔治疑似老年虐待问题发生争议。苏茜·萨洛还向 FDA 顾问委员会提交了公开意见，警告称“MDMA 在被视为剥削工具方面已是公认的用途”。当被问及苏茜·萨洛的资金贡献时，诺曼坚持称 Psymposia 是“没人想象中的谁的攻击犬”。德韦诺（Devenot）指出，Psymposia 的成员多年前就已经多年反复研究并提出同样的担忧，远早于获得这笔资助。

paragraphs.en: In the view of some MAPS-trained therapists, Psymposia had leveraged the Buisson incident to discredit the entire treatment. Devenot’s complaint to the FDA states that its protocols “incentivize” exploitative dynamics between vulnerable patients and potentially predatory practitioners. Abusive therapists like Yensen and Dryer, Devenot alleged on Substack last fall, are “an inevitable consequence of MAPS’s therapeutic ideology.” (Speaking on behalf of Psymposia, Devenot responds that "We are disappointed to see that MAPS has reverted to scapegoating critics rather than engaging with substantive safety improvements that would protect future patients.")
paragraphs.zh: 在一些接受 MAPS 训练的治疗师看来，Psymposia 借助布桑（Buisson）事件来抹黑整个疗法。德韦诺在致 FDA 的投诉中写道，Psymposia 的治疗方案“助长”了脆弱患者与可能带有掠夺倾向的临床实践者之间的剥削性关系。德韦诺在去年秋天的 Substack 上指控，像 Yensen 与 Dryer 这类施虐型治疗师“是 MAPS 治疗理念的必然产物”。（代表 Psymposia 发言时，德韦诺回应道： “我们很失望看到 MAPS 又一次退回到替罪羊逻辑，而不是投入实质性的安全改进，以保护未来的患者。”）

paragraphs.en: “The metaphor they use is that’s not just one rotten apple, it’s the whole orchard,” says Casey Paleos, a board-certified psychiatrist who served as a therapist in MAPS’s Phase 3 trial. “I know the other apples in the orchard. None of us are like Richard Yensen.”
paragraphs.zh: “他们用的比喻是——这可不只是一个坏苹果，而是整片果园，”在 MAPS 第三期试验中担任治疗师的持牌精神科医生凯西·帕莱奥斯（Casey Paleos）说。“我认识果园里的其他苹果。我们没有一个人像理查德·延森（Richard Yensen）那样。”

paragraphs.en: I interviewed several patients from the MAPS Phase 3 trial to get a detailed sense of how the therapy typically unfolds in a clinical setting. Laura Lynn MacDonald, a 55-year-old mother of two, is a survivor of childhood sexual abuse. She had cycled in and out of therapy and been prescribed antidepressants and a battery of antianxiety medications to treat panic attacks, ADHD, and insomnia. (The average trial participant had been living with PTSD for 14 years.)
paragraphs.zh: 我采访了多位来自 MAPS 第三期试验的患者，以更细致地了解这种治疗在临床场景中通常如何展开。55 岁、育有两名子女的劳拉·琳·麦克唐纳（Laura Lynn MacDonald）是童年性虐待的幸存者。她反复进出心理治疗，曾被开抗抑郁药，并接受过一系列抗焦虑药，以治疗惊恐发作、多动症和失眠。（试验中平均每位参与者已与 PTSD 共处 14 年。）

paragraphs.en: MacDonald credits the treatment with curing her symptoms. By her account, the therapists were methodical and responsible as they helped her process intense sensations and memories. Throughout, she felt she was being safely guided, even flashing back to a very vivid, very fond memory of being paddled in a canoe by two caring camp counselors. “No boundaries were crossed. Safety measures were diligently followed,” she said, adding, “as a sexual abuse survivor, the manipulation and weaponization of the only sexual abuse case that occurred during the MAPS clinical trials infuriated me.”
paragraphs.zh: 麦克唐纳（MacDonald）把自己的症状缓解归功于这项治疗。她表示，治疗师们在帮助她处理强烈的感受和记忆时，非常有条理且负责任。她始终觉得自己是在被安全地引导着，甚至一度闪现出一段非常清晰、非常温馨的记忆：两位关怀备至的营地辅导员在独木舟里为她划桨。她说：“没有越界。安全措施被认真地落实了。”并补充道：“作为性虐待幸存者，在 MAPS 临床试验中发生的那起唯一性虐待案件被人操纵并加以武器化，这件事让我非常愤怒。”

paragraphs.en: During the hearing, Devenot used the account of a therapist who pinned down a patient to illustrate a wider pattern of abuse. The therapist, Veronika Gold, says that the patient (who took ketamine, not MDMA) had consented to touch and role-play and that Gold was in character as the patient’s abusive father. The patient has never spoken publicly about the event, and Gold quoted this case of a “reparative fantasy” extensively in a book chapter she wrote. “It has been very upsetting,” Gold says of Devenot’s characterization of her work. “They are misrepresenting what actually happened … and it seems like it made an impact on the advisory committee." Devenot, meanwhile, contends that Gold’s use of physical touch “exposes patients to an undisclosed risk of retraumatization.”
paragraphs.zh: 在听证会上，德弗诺（Devenot）借一位治疗师的个案来说明更广泛的虐待模式。该治疗师弗罗妮卡·戈德（Veronika Gold）称，患者（当时使用的是氯胺酮，而非 MDMA）曾同意触碰和角色扮演，而戈德在其中扮演患者的施虐父亲。患者从未公开谈及这起事件，而戈德则在她写的一章书中大量引用了这个“修复性幻想”的案例。戈德对德弗诺对她工作的表述说：“这让我非常不安。他们歪曲了实际发生的事情……而且似乎对咨询委员会产生了影响。”与此同时，德弗诺认为，戈德的身体接触“让患者面临一种未被披露的再创伤化风险”。

paragraphs.en: It’s unknown exactly how Psymposia’s criticisms factored into the FDA’s final decision. Members of the FDA advisory committee did not respond to repeated requests for interviews. I spoke to two former Lykos executives who had seen the agency’s Complete Response Letter, who said that the FDA treated Psymposia’s concerns as significant and that the agency cited issues with trial design, a consistent bugbear of psychedelic treatments.The FDA also allegedly flagged an underreporting of “positive adverse events” in which the experience proved not just therapeutic, but so enjoyable that a patient might be primed to abuse the drug.
paragraphs.zh: Psymposia 的批评到底在 FDA 最终裁决中起了多大作用尚不清楚。FDA 咨询委员会的成员未回应反复提出的采访请求。我采访了两位曾见过该机构《完全回应信》（Complete Response Letter）的前 Lykos 高管，他们表示 FDA 将 Psymposia 的担忧视为重要问题，并指出该机构提及了试验设计上的缺陷——这是精神药物治疗长期存在的常见症结。FDA 还被指称点出了“阳性不良事件”存在低报现象，即治疗体验不仅具备治疗作用，而且十分愉快，以致患者可能被“预先引导”去滥用药物。

paragraphs.en: In the aftermath of the ruling, Psymposia certainly seemed happy to claim credit for the outcome. The group commissioned a PR firm, which pitched journalists on the story of a “small watchdog organization that stopped a billionaire-funded company, Lykos Therapeutics, from getting their unscientific and flawed MDMA therapy model approved by the FDA.” Last fall, Devenot posted a picture on Substack of them posing in front of an FDA flag, with the caption “My Everest.”
paragraphs.zh: 判决作出后，Psymposia 显然很乐于把结果功劳归于自己。该组织委托了一家公关公司，并向记者推介了一个故事：一个“体量不大的监察机构阻止了一家亿万富翁资助的公司、Lykos Therapeutics，将其未经科学验证、存在缺陷的 MDMA 疗法模型提交 FDA 并获批。”去年秋天，德弗诺在 Substack 上发布了一张照片，照片里他们站在一面 FDA 旗帜前，并配文“我的珠穆朗玛峰”。

paragraphs.en: Strangely serene about his life’s work being kneecapped, Rick Doblin lays the blame as much with Psymposia as with the Lykos brass. In the run-up to the FDA hearing, Lykos management instituted a mandatory “quiet period,” preventing anyone involved with the drug application from responding to criticism. In his mind, this created a sort of vacuum that his critics eagerly and capably filled. “I think they deserve full credit,” says Doblin wryly, of Psymposia. “Now, deserve doesn’t mean that I think what they did was right. Just that they were very effective.”
paragraphs.zh: 面对自己毕生事业被打断，Rick Doblin 却出奇地平静，他把责任同样归在 Psymposia 身上，也归在 Lykos 的高层身上。在 FDA 听证会前，Lykos 管理层实行了强制性“静默期”，不允许任何参与药物申请的人对外回应批评。在他看来，这就形成了一种“空白”，而他的批评者正好又聪明又积极地填补了这个空白。“我认为他们值得给予全部功劳，”Doblin 带着讽刺意味地评价 Psymposia。 “不过，值得功劳并不代表我认为他们做得对，只是他们做得非常有效。”

paragraphs.en: After the FDA setback, Doblin relinquished his seat on Lykos’ board of directors. The company slashed three-quarters of its staff, and scientists and stakeholders from Big Pharma heavyweights and other multinationals were shuffled in.
paragraphs.zh: 在 FDA 的挫败之后，Doblin 辞去了在 Lykos 董事会中的席位。公司裁掉了四分之三的员工，并且引进了来自大型制药公司和其他跨国企业的科学家与利益相关者。

paragraphs.en: Yet Doblin persists. He recently helped to secure Lykos $25 million from billionaire and longtime Elon Musk ally Antonio Gracias, an early Tesla and SpaceX investor who also financially backed Musk’s pro-Trump Super PAC and now works as part of DOGE efforts in the federal government. Doblin hung out with Gracias, who has also funded psychedelic research at Harvard and London’s Imperial College, at a predawn Burning Man party late last summer. (In one sense, Gracias makes an unusual ally; Doblin’s daughter lost her job due to government cuts enacted by DOGE.)
paragraphs.zh: 然而，Doblin 依然在坚持。近期他帮助 Lykos 获得了 2500 万美元资金，出资人是亿万富翁、长期的 Elon Musk 盟友 Antonio Gracias——这位早期的 Tesla 和 SpaceX 投资者也为 Musk 的亲川（pro-Trump）超级政治行动委员会提供过财务支持，并且现在参与联邦政府的 DOGE 工作。Doblin 曾在去年的晚夏，与 Gracias 在破晓前于 Burning Man 的一场派对上一起度过夜晚。（从某种意义上说，Gracias 是一位不寻常的盟友；Doblin 的女儿因 DOGE 推动的政府削减而失去了工作。）

paragraphs.en: In March, Lykos received additional support from Gracias and Sir Christopher Hohn, a British hedge fund manager and philanthropist who has been described by The New York Times as “one of Europe’s most feared activist investors.” A seasoned pharma executive, Michael Burke, is stepping in as the company’s new CEO. Lykos declined to make any members of its new leadership team available for comment.
paragraphs.zh: 3 月份，Lykos 又得到了 Gracias 和英国对冲基金经理、慈善家 Christopher Hohn（其曾被《纽约时报》形容为“欧洲最让人害怕的积极主义投资者”之一）的进一步支持。经验丰富的制药高管 Michael Burke 正接任公司新任首席执行官。Lykos 拒绝让其新管理团队中的任何成员接受采访。

paragraphs.en: At the time of writing, Lykos plans to use its new investment to submit the MDMA application to the FDA again, in hope that the new MAHA regime will be more inclined to reexamine the data. An approval of the therapy, if granted, would likely require a Phase 4 study in which the treatment is carefully monitored after it has hit the market. But if the FDA refuses the reapplication, Lykos would need to run another Phase 3 study, conduct years of further research, and expend many more millions. A spokesperson for HHS says “the agency is actively exploring and supporting innovative treatment options,” including “emerging approaches using gold standard science.”
paragraphs.zh: 在撰写本文时，Lykos 计划用其新一轮投资再次向美国食品药品监督管理局（FDA）提交 MDMA 的申请，希望新的 MAHA 机制会更愿意重新审视这些数据。如果该疗法获得批准，可能还需要进行一项四期研究，在其上市后对治疗进行严密监测。然而，如果 FDA 拒绝再次申请，Lykos 将不得不再做一项三期研究，进行数年的进一步研究，并投入更多数以百万计的资金。HHS 的一位发言人表示，“该机构正在积极探索并支持创新治疗方案”，其中包括“基于黄金标准科学的创新方法。”

paragraphs.en: In the meantime, Doblin and Psymposia have made efforts to cool hostilities. In March, Doblin, Devenot, and Normand met up at the South by Southwest conference in Austin. Over the course of around four hours, Doblin listened to their questions and criticisms of the MDMA therapy. Devenot says Doblin treated them as “good-faith actors; people who had genuine concerns but were not saboteurs.” Doblin, too, spoke warmly of the meeting. “They just want to protect vulnerable people,” he says. “We all gave each other a hug after the meeting.” (Neither Devenot nor Normand would confirm or deny the hug.)
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，Doblin 和 Psymposia 已努力缓和对立。3 月，Doblin、Devenot 和 Normand 在德克萨斯州奥斯汀的南方偏东（South by Southwest）会议上会面。会议持续了大约四个小时，Doblin 听取了他们对 MDMA 治疗的提问与批评。Devenot 说，Doblin 把他们当作“善意的参与者”；这些人“有真实的担忧，但并非破坏者”。Doblin 也对这次会面评价很高。“他们只是想保护脆弱的人群，”他表示。“我们在会后彼此拥抱。”（Devenot 和 Normand 都既未确认也未否认这次拥抱。）

paragraphs.en: For patients who have benefitted from the therapy, all the infighting seems rather academic. The ones I spoke to also weren’t troubled by the optics of partnering with Trump- or Musk-aligned allies. Laura Lynn MacDonald told me she didn’t care who the “delivery man” was for “the good medicine”—she just wanted the treatment to be available for people who needed it. The interest in psychedelics from MAHA-world, she observed, optimistically, “might be a strange perfection of ingredients.”
paragraphs.zh: 对于已经从这种疗法中受益的患者来说，所有内耗看起来都比较学术化。我采访过的人也并不在意与支持特朗普或马斯克的盟友合作带来的外部观感。Laura Lynn MacDonald 告诉我，她并不在乎“好药”的“递送者”是谁——她只希望这种治疗能让需要的人用得上。她观察到，MAHA 世界里的迷幻疗法兴趣，乐观地说，“可能是一种奇妙的成分配比。”

paragraphs.en: Let us know what you think about this article. Submit a letter to the editor at [email protected].
paragraphs.zh: 欢迎告诉我们您对这篇文章的看法。请向[电子邮件保护]投稿给本报记者。
